payload.text:
I am the organiser for a bbq festival called FUME (logo attached). This year, we have some Americans coming to the UK to cook. It is really important that we get them onboarded as simply as possible, since we don't want to scare them off by doing too much work. 

To that effect, I need to create an "Onboarding App" that gathers the critical information in a gamified way - i.e. using buttons instead of text boxes where possible. 

The entered information should upload to a google sheets back end, so be sure to include the necessary Apps Script for this.

The critical information needed are:
Menu (Dish, description, meat specs, meat quantities, date needed, other ingredient specs, other ingredients quantities, date needed)
Equipment (Item, item spec, quantity needed, photo example, date needed)
Fuel (Fuel type, Fuel specification, quantity needed, date needed)
Cooking Process (Date, process (e.g. kitchen prep and start smoke followed by vacuum seal), result, generic requirements (e.g. kitchen, smoker))
Staff (Full Names, passport number, passport photo, DoB, Address, Is supplementary staff required from FUME?)
Flights (Number of flights required, Desired depature airport 1(USA-UK), desired depature date 1(USA-UK), Desired depature airport 2(UK-USA), desired depature date 2(UK-USA))

Remember - Gamify this as much as possible and make the app look very smart with the FUME logo dotted around. 
Traders need to be able to make edits easily

**Designing the BBQ Onboarding**

I'm focusing on the React application for onboarding US BBQ teams for the FUME festival. My key goal is a gamified experience with minimal text, heavily reliant on buttons and toggles. I'll prioritize a placeholder for the FUME logo, and am deciding between a high-quality placeholder or an SVG for optimal scaling and performance.


**Refining the Onboarding Structure**

I'm now prioritizing the Google Sheets integration via Apps Script to capture team data. My immediate focus is on structuring the wizard steps: Menu, Equipment, Fuel, Cooking Process, Staff, and Flights. I'm leaning toward visual cards and quick-select chips to streamline data input. I'm also planning the `metadata.json` for name, description, and camera permissions, along with the `index.html` file using Tailwind and Lucide icons.

payload.text:
After the welcome page, add an info page:
"FUME 2026 is being held at the Allianz Stadium, Twickenham, London. 

The live dates are Thursday 11th to Sunday 14th of June. Site access (including kitchen, smoking and wash up space) is available from Monday 8th of June.

IMPORTANT!
There are 5000 people coming on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday. Sunday will see about 3500 visitors. These are hungry guests who are desperate to try your food, so when planning menus, please consider ingredients ordering, speed of service and simplicity to make your lives easier.

If anything is unclear on this form, please email dan@savourfestival and alex@savourfestival, or use the whatsapp group for clarity."

On the menu, under meat requirements please add "Weight per cut" and "Total Quantity needed" and "Portion per day Target". Make the primary protein list more extensive. For the sides &other ingredients, make it so you add an item with the quantity required. Clarify that the date box is the date needed on site for cooking.

On the gear, make the item list more extensive. Make it far more obvious that the item specification needs to be filled in by them. Make it clearer that the date is when they require it at site.

Change the fuel item list to: "Wood, Gas, Charcoal, Briquettes, Pellets". Change the quantity to kg/lbs total

On staffing, make it so that they can add a photo of the passport and add a "Role on site" section with buttons for "Chef, Pitmaster, Front of House, Back of House, Family". Add check box for "Flight needed". Move the "Need Extra Hands?" outside of the member boxes to the top of the page, and if selected yes make a drop down with a quantity of 1-6.

On the flights page, make an description box saying all staff flights must depart from the same airport. Then remove the number of flights required so that it is just one of each. Change the return airport to ask for the US arrival airport.

On the review page, show a longer, clean outline of all submitted fields

**Developing the BBQ App**

I'm focusing on the FUME BBQ Festival onboarding app updates. A new information page will follow the introduction. The menu requires significant changes, including adding fields for weight per cut, total quantity needed, and portion targets, with an extensive protein list. I am improving the side items and quantities, and clarifying date labels.


**Refining the Onboarding Process**

I've been working on significant updates, particularly to the Gear and Fuel sections. The Gear list now has a clearer spec field and clarified date labels. Fuel details are more specific now, with quantities in kg/lbs. The Staffing section adds a passport photo field and role selection via buttons, plus a "flight needed" checkbox. I've re-focused the "Need Extra Hands" logic and updated the flight handling to assume one round trip per staff. Also, the review page has a better outline. I'm updating the interface types and constants for those changes. I'm adding an info step for the app.

### How to Edit the Gallery Photos

Currently, the showcase photos on the **Info (Stadium)**, **Kitchen**, and **Stay (Hotel)** pages are controlled by three constants at the top of your `App.tsx` file.

To change them:
1.  **Find the constants**: Look at the top of `App.tsx` (around lines 90-110) for `STADIUM_IMAGES`, `KITCHEN_IMAGES`, and `HOTEL_IMAGES`.
2.  **Replace the URLs**: You can replace the existing links with your own hosted image URLs (e.g., from Unsplash, Imgur, or your own website). 
3.  **Using your own files**: You cannot "upload" files directly into the code like a text field. You first need to host the images online and then copy the "Image Link" into the array in the code.
    *   *Alternative:* If the images are small, you could convert them to a "Base64 string" and paste that directly into the quotes, though hosted URLs are much cleaner for the code.

For example, to update the stadium photos, you would edit this section in `App.tsx`:

```typescript
const STADIUM_IMAGES = [
  "https://your-link-to-photo-1.jpg",
  "https://your-link-to-photo-2.jpg",
  "https://your-link-to-photo-3.jpg"
];
```

The app will automatically resize and apply the premium "grayscale to color" hover effect to whatever links you provide!

**Analyzing Passport Integration**

I'm currently focused on explaining the impact of incorporating the passport expiry date field into the "Crew" page, aiming to outline necessary backend adjustments (Apps Script). I'm striving to detail these without modifying existing code, a key constraint I need to navigate.


**Clarifying Backend Adjustments**

I'm now zeroing in on the specific backend modifications needed within the Apps Script to accommodate the new `passportExpiry` field. My aim is to clearly illustrate the mapping required – how to incorporate the field into the existing `doPost` function and how the script needs to update the "Staff" sheet. This involves updating data mapping and sheet structure, and ensuring the `appendRow` call accounts for this new column.

Adding the **Passport Expiry Date** field creates a small "mismatch" between your web app and your Google Sheet. To fix this and ensure the data is captured correctly, you need to make two specific updates to your backend environment:

### 1. Update the Google Sheet (The "Bucket")
Since you are now collecting an extra piece of information for every crew member, your **"Staff"** (or "Crew") tab in Google Sheets needs a place to store it.
*   **Action:** Go to your Google Sheet, find the **"Staff"** tab, and insert a new column for **"Passport Expiry"**.
*   **Placement:** It is best to place it right after the "Passport #" column so the data flows logically.

### 2. Update the Apps Script (The "Bridge")
Your Apps Script is responsible for taking the data from the web app and "mapping" it to the correct column. Currently, your script's `appendRow` function for staff members doesn't know the `passportExpiry` field exists.

**You need to update the `doPost` function in your Apps Script to include the new field:**

Find the section in your Apps Script that handles staff (usually looks like `data.staff.forEach`) and update the `appendRow` line to look like this:

```javascript
// Process Staff
const staffSheet = getOrCreateSheet(ss, "Staff");
data.staff.forEach(person => {
  staffSheet.appendRow([
    new Date(), 
    data.teamName, 
    person.fullName, 
    person.passportNumber, 
    person.passportExpiry, // <--- ADD THIS LINE
    person.dob, 
    person.address, 
    data.needsSupplementaryStaff ? "Yes" : "No"
  ]);
});
```

### Summary of Impact
*   **Data Integrity:** If you **don't** update the script, the expiry date will simply be ignored and won't appear in your spreadsheet.
*   **Column Alignment:** If you add the column to the sheet but forget to update the script, the script will continue to put the "Date of Birth" into the "Passport Expiry" column, causing all your data to "shift" one cell to the left.
*   **Sync:** Once the script and the sheet headers match the order of the items in that `appendRow` array, the sync will be perfect.

**Important Note:** After you update the code in the Apps Script editor, you **must** click **"Deploy" > "New Deployment"** again to ensure the changes take effect. Using the old URL or an old version of the script will result in the new field being missed.
